proofs.proof_tex:
Proof. Fix nu in N(pbar) and consider any sequence of finite two-stage randomized-saturation designs whose cluster saturation vectors pi have empirical laws L_M(pi) converging weakly to nu. Under ass:equal-cluster-size the clusters have common size m, so the cluster-level treatment-proportion vector pi is the object entering the randomized-saturation variance expansion. Under ass:isolated-partial-interference and ass:anonymous-share-specialization, the design contribution of assignments outside a cluster and all within-cluster assignment features other than the saturation share are absent from the leading criterion. Under ass:homogeneous-linear-share-working-model and ass:cai-leading-expansion-domain, the block-fixed isolated specialization is in the scope of Cai, Pouget-Abadie, and Airoldi's randomized-saturation expansion recorded in lem:cai-randomized-saturation-moment-expansion, with the V_2 coefficient equal to zero. Thus the o(N^{-1})-free leading term for a finite saturation vector pi is

V_0 + V_1 mu_2(pi) + V_3 mu_3(pi) + V_4{mu_4(pi)-mu_2(pi)^2},

where mu_r(pi)=M^{-1} sum_{j=1}^M (pi_j-pbar)^r for r=2,3,4. Since L_M(pi) converges weakly to nu and each function u -> (u-pbar)^r is continuous and bounded on [0,1], lem:empirical-centered-moment-continuity gives mu_r(pi) -> m_r(nu) for r=2,3,4. Passing to the law-level relaxation therefore gives the limiting o(N^{-1})-free leading criterion

V_0 + V_1 m_2(nu) + V_3 m_3(nu) + V_4{m_4(nu)-m_2(nu)^2}.

By def:variance-functional this expression is exactly V(nu). Hence, within this homogeneous isolated-share specialization, first-stage saturation optimization for the standard Cai leading design-variance criterion is precisely minimization of the quartic moment functional V over N(pbar). The assertion is only about the leading criterion after dropping the o(N^{-1}) remainder; the exact non-leading finite-population variance is not identified with V and remains outside this criterion-level statement.

Proof. Under ass:equal-cluster-size, ass:isolated-partial-interference, ass:anonymous-share-specialization, ass:homogeneous-linear-share-working-model, and ass:cai-leading-expansion-domain, lem:variance-moment-reduction identifies the standard o(N^{-1})-free leading randomized-saturation variance criterion associated with any admissible law nu as V(nu). Therefore a law is leading-design-variance-optimal over N(pbar) if and only if it minimizes V over N(pbar). The minimizers of V are characterized exactly by thm:global-certificate, so the same certificate characterizes leading design-variance optimality in the model regime of the bridge lemma.

If 0<pbar<1, prop:positive-penalty-threshold applies to the pure moment program and gives

delta_pbar in argmin_{nu in N(pbar)} V(nu) if and only if min_{d in D_pbar} (V_1+V_3 d+V_4 d^2) >= 0.

Combining this equivalence with the preceding leading-criterion identification gives the stated if and only if for leading-design-variance optimality of the stratified law delta_pbar. If pbar=0 or pbar=1, lem:boundary-admissible-law-singleton gives N(pbar)={delta_pbar}, and delta_pbar is automatically leading-design-variance-optimal. Finally, when V_3=V_4=0, prop:sutva-corner gives the SUTVA degeneration of the same V-minimization problem, including the stratified, Bernoulli-vertex, tie, and finite-compatibility clauses. Since the bridge lemma deliberately identifies only the leading o(N^{-1})-free criterion, the exact non-leading finite-population variance remains a separate scope item.

added_lemmas.statement: Cai-Pouget-Abadie-Airoldi randomized-saturation variance expansion. For an equal-cluster randomized-saturation design under the linear interference model and the regularity regime M -> infinity and inf_j N_j -> infinity used in Cai, Pouget-Abadie, and Airoldi (2022), if pi is the cluster treatment-proportion vector with mean pbar, mu_2(pi) is its centered second moment, and mu_3(pi), mu_4(pi) are its centered third and fourth moments, then Var_Z[hat_tau] = V_0 + V_1 mu_2(pi) + V_2 mu_2(pi)^2 + V_3 mu_3(pi) + V_4 (mu_4(pi) - mu_2(pi)^2) + o(N^-1). Under the block-fixed isolated-cluster specialization, the coefficient V_2 is zero.
added_lemmas.proof_tex: This is the published variance-expansion substrate from Cai, Pouget-Abadie, and Airoldi (2022), Theorem 9 together with the randomized-saturation specialization summarized in their Corollary 12. Those results give the central-moment expansion of the design variance for randomized saturation designs under the linear interference model in the stated asymptotic regime. In the block-fixed isolated-cluster specialization, the cross-cluster/interference component generating the V_2 mu_2(pi)^2 term is absent, so the specialization has V_2=0. This citation is used only as the classical leading-expansion input; the present note's contribution is the bounded-support quartic moment optimization built from that input.

added_lemmas.statement: Let pi^(M) be saturation vectors in [0,1]^M whose empirical laws L_M(pi^(M)) converge weakly to nu in P([0,1]), and let pbar be fixed. Then for r in {2,3,4}, M^(-1) sum_{j=1}^M (pi_j^(M)-pbar)^r -> integral_[0,1] (u-pbar)^r d nu(u). In particular, if nu in N(pbar), the limit is m_r(nu).
added_lemmas.proof_tex: For each r in {2,3,4}, the function g_r(u)=(u-pbar)^r is continuous and bounded on the compact interval [0,1]. Weak convergence L_M(pi^(M)) => nu therefore implies integral g_r dL_M(pi^(M)) -> integral g_r dnu. By def:empirical-law, integral g_r dL_M(pi^(M)) = M^{-1} sum_{j=1}^M (pi_j^(M)-pbar)^r. If nu lies in N(pbar), def:centered-moments identifies the limiting integral with m_r(nu).

Let nu be a probability law on [0,1] with integral u dnu(u)=pbar. If pbar=0, then u>=0 on [0,1] and a nonnegative random variable with mean zero is zero almost surely; hence nu=delta_0. If pbar=1, then 1-u>=0 and integral (1-u)dnu(u)=1-integral u dnu(u)=0, so 1-u=0 almost surely and nu=delta_1. The reverse inclusions delta_0 in N(0) and delta_1 in N(1) are immediate from the same mean constraint in def:admissible-law-class.
